subject: Geography
title: Urban Heat Islands
description: A micro-lesson that makes Urban Heat Islands usable.

hook.text: Cities can be 5–10°C hotter than surrounding countryside—a phenomenon called the heat island effect.

buildup.text: Concrete, asphalt, and glass absorb heat all day and radiate it back at night.

discovery.text: Trees and parks cool cities by providing shade and releasing moisture into the air.

twist.text: Poor neighborhoods often have fewer trees and more concrete—making them the hottest zones.

climax.text: As climate change raises temperatures, urban heat islands turn deadly for vulnerable residents.

punchline.text: The concrete jungle is literally hotter than the actual jungle.

quiz.question: What causes the urban heat island effect?
options: Concrete and asphalt absorbing and radiating heat
